channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Black Hat USA
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: [Webinar] How to Close Identity Gaps in 2026 Before AI Exploits Enterprise Risk
item.description: In the rapid evolution of the 2026 threat landscape, a frustrating paradox has emerged for CISOs and security leaders: Identity programs are maturing, yet the risk is actually increasing. According to...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Recurring Credential Incidents
item.description: When talking about credential security, the focus usually lands on breach prevention. This makes sense when IBM’s 2025 Cost of a Data Breach Report puts the average cost of a breach at $4.4 million. A...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New GPUBreach Attack Enables Full CPU Privilege Escalation via GDDR6 Bit-Flips
item.description: New academic research has identified multiple RowHammer attacks against high-performance graphics processing units (GPUs) that could be exploited to escalate privileges and, in some cases, even take f...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: China-Linked Storm-1175 Exploits Zero-Days to Rapidly Deploy Medusa Ransomware
item.description: A China-based threat actor known for deploying Medusa ransomware has been linked to the weaponization of a combination of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities to orchestrate "high-velocity" attacks and ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Flowise AI Agent Builder Under Active CVSS 10.0 RCE Exploitation; 12,000+ Instances Exposed
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw in Flowise, an open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform, according to new findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: German authorities identify REvil and GandCrab ransomware bosses
item.description: The Federal Police in Germany (BKA) has identified two Russian nationals as the leaders of GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations between 2019 and 2021. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New GPUBreach attack enables system takeover via GPU rowhammer
item.description: A new attack, dubbed GPUBreach, can induce Rowhammer bit-flips on GPU GDDR6 memories to escalate privileges and lead to a full system compromise. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI-Assisted Supply Chain Attack Targets GitHub
item.description: PRT-scan is the second in recent months where a threat actor appears to have leveraged AI for automated targeting of a widespread GitHub misconfiguration....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Axios Attack Shows Social Complex Engineering Is Industrialized
item.description: The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Fortinet Issues Emergency Patch for FortiClient Zero-Day
item.description: The authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-35616, is the latest in a series of Fortinet vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Disgruntled researcher leaks “BlueHammer” Windows zero-day exploit
item.description: Exploit code has been released for an unpatched Windows privilege escalation flaw reported privately to Microsoft, allowing attackers to gain SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft fixes Classic Outlook bug causing email delivery issues
item.description: Microsoft has resolved a known issue that was preventing some Classic Outlook users from sending emails via Outlook.com. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Iran-Linked Password-Spraying Campaign Targets 300+ Israeli Microsoft 365 Organizations
item.description: An Iran-nexus threat actor is suspected to be behind a password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments in Israel and the U.A.E. amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The activity, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft removes Support and Recovery Assistant from Windows
item.description: Microsoft has deprecated and removed the Support and Recovery Assistant (SaRA) command-line utility from all in-support versions of Windows updates starting March 10. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft links Medusa ransomware affiliate to zero-day attacks
item.description: Microsoft says that Storm-1175, a China-based financially motivated cybercriminal group known for deploying Medusa ransomware payloads, has been deploying n-day and zero-day exploits in high-velocity ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Drift $280M crypto theft linked to 6-month in-person operation
item.description: The Drift Protocol says that the $280+ million hack it suffered last week was the result of a long-term, carefully planned operation that included building "a functioning operational presence inside t...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DPRK-Linked Hackers Use GitHub as C2 in Multi-Stage Attacks Targeting South Korea
item.description: Threat actors likely associated with the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) have been observed using GitHub as command-and-control (C2) infrastructure in multi-stage attacks targeting organi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch exploited Fortinet EMS flaw by Friday
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered federal agencies to secure FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) instances against an actively exploited vulnerability...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Automated Credential Harvesting Campaign Exploits React2Shell Flaw
item.description: An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Shadow AI in Healthcare Is Here to Stay
item.description: Medical professionals are not going to stop using AI tools to manage growing workloads. Organizations should prioritize bolstering security protocols to limit their blast radius....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Why Simple Breach Monitoring is No Longer Enough
item.description: Infostealers are harvesting credentials and session cookies at scale, bypassing traditional defenses. Lunar explains why simple breach monitoring alone can't keep up with modern credential-based attac...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OWASP GenAI Security Project Gets Update, New Tools Matrix
item.description: In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards groups recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Multi-OS Cyberattacks: How SOCs Close a Critical Risk in 3 Steps
item.description: Your attack surface no longer lives on one operating system, and neither do the campaigns targeting it. In enterprise environments, attackers move across Windows endpoints, executive MacBooks, Linux i...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Axios Hack, Chrome 0-Day, Fortinet Exploits, Paragon Spyware and More
item.description: This week had real hits. The key software got tampered with. Active bugs showed up in the tools people use every day. Some attacks didn’t even need much effort because the path was already there. One ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: How LiteLLM Turned Developer Machines Into Credential Vaults for Attackers
item.description: The most active piece of enterprise infrastructure in the company is the developer workstation. That laptop is where credentials are created, tested, cached, copied, and reused across services, bots, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Qilin and Warlock Ransomware Use Vulnerable Drivers to Disable 300+ EDR Tools
item.description: Threat actors associated with Qilin and Warlock ransomware operations have been observed using the bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) technique to silence security tools running on compromised h...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: BKA Identifies REvil Leaders Behind 130 German Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (aka BKA or the Bundeskriminalamt) has unmasked the real identities of two of the key figures associated with the now-defunct REvil (aka Sodinokibi) ransomware...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimov...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Traffic violation scams switch to QR codes in new phishing texts
item.description: Scammers are sending fake "Notice of Default" traffic violation text messages impersonating state courts across the U.S., pressuring recipients to scan a QR code that leads to a phishing site demandin...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
